chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

text: 2664 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَضَّأَنِي اَلْغُلاَمُ وَ أَنَا لاَ أَعْلَمُ بدستشان فِيهِ طِيبٌ فَغَسَلْتُ يَدَيَّ وَ أَنَا مُحْرِمٌ فَقَالَ «تَصَدَّقْ بِشَيْءٍ لِذَلِكَ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2664 - It was narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Ziyad, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "My servant poured water for me to perform ablution, and I was unaware that there was fragrance (dustashan) in it. I then washed my hands while I was in Ihram."
Imam (as) said: "Give something in charity as an expiation for that."
